title: My favorite weekend
summary: Describe what you usually do on weekends using present simple and adverbs of frequency.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about what you usually do on weekends. Use present simple and adverbs of frequency (always, usually, sometimes).
hintStarters: I always wake up late because…
sampleText: On weekends, I always sleep in and wake up at eight or nine. I live in a small flat in Lisbon, and the streets are quiet on Saturday mornings. I usually go to the market near my building. The market sells fresh vegetables, bread, and fruit. I sometimes buy oranges and tomatoes because they are cheap and very good. I carry the bag home and I cook a simple meal. I always wash the dishes after lunch. In the afternoon, I usually meet my friends at a coffee shop near the river. We drink coffee and talk for a long time. Sometimes we go for a walk by the water and we sit on a park bench. The air is cool and fresh. I feel relaxed and happy. In the evening, I sometimes tidy up my flat. I put on some music and I clean the kitchen and the floor. I never work on Sundays because I need to rest. On Sunday evening, I usually read a book or watch a short film. This is my favorite time of the week.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chợ gần tòa nhà tôi ở
nấu một bữa ăn đơn giản

sampleAnnotations.phrase: go for a walk by the water

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm thấy thư giãn và vui vẻ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: my favorite time of the week
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thời gian yêu thích nhất trong tuần

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for regular actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn cho những việc làm thường xuyên vào cuối tuần: 'I go', 'I cook', 'I meet'. Với chủ ngữ 'I' không thêm đuôi -s.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for regular weekend activities: 'I go', 'I cook', 'I meet'. With the subject 'I', the verb takes no -s ending.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'always', 'usually', 'sometimes', 'never' đặt trước động từ chính: 'I always sleep in', 'I usually go', 'I sometimes buy', 'I never work'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'always', 'usually', 'sometimes', and 'never' go before the main verb: 'I always sleep in', 'I usually go to the market'.

sampleTranslationVi: Vào cuối tuần, tôi luôn ngủ nướng và thức dậy lúc tám hoặc chín giờ. Tôi sống trong một căn hộ nhỏ ở Lisbon, và các con phố rất yên tĩnh vào sáng thứ Bảy. Tôi thường đi chợ gần tòa nhà tôi ở. Chợ bán rau củ tươi, bánh mì và trái cây. Đôi khi tôi mua cam và cà chua vì chúng rẻ và rất ngon. Tôi xách túi về nhà và nấu một bữa ăn đơn giản. Tôi luôn rửa bát sau bữa trưa. Buổi chiều, tôi thường gặp bạn bè ở một quán cà phê gần sông. Chúng tôi uống cà phê và nói chuyện lâu. Đôi khi chúng tôi đi dạo ven sông và ngồi trên ghế công viên. Không khí mát mẻ và trong lành. Tôi cảm thấy thư giãn và vui vẻ. Buổi tối, đôi khi tôi dọn dẹp căn hộ. Tôi bật nhạc và lau dọn bếp và sàn nhà. Tôi không bao giờ làm việc vào Chủ Nhật vì tôi cần nghỉ ngơi. Tối Chủ Nhật, tôi thường đọc sách hoặc xem một bộ phim ngắn. Đây là khoảng thời gian yêu thích nhất trong tuần của tôi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: I go always to the market on Saturday.
Always I go to the market on Saturday.
I always go to the market on Saturday.
I go to always the market on Saturday.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverbs of frequency go before the main verb: 'I always go'.
mcQuestions.hint: The adverb sits between the subject and the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is in the present simple and describes a habit?
options: I was cooking a meal last weekend.
I am cooking a meal right now.
I cook a simple meal every weekend.
I will cook a meal next week.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple with 'every weekend' shows a regular habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a time phrase that shows repetition.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'sometimes' in the correct position?
options: Sometimes I meet friends at a café.
I meet sometimes friends at a café.
I meet friends sometimes always.
Sometimes meeting friends café I.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Sometimes' is correct at the beginning of a sentence for emphasis.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Sometimes' is fine at the start of a sentence or before the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'She ___ the dishes after lunch.'
options: wash
washes
washing
washed
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person singular adds -es to 'wash': 'she washes'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the subject 'she' — it changes the verb ending.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which linker shows contrast between two ideas?
options: I meet friends and we drink coffee.
I meet friends so we drink coffee.
I meet friends because we drink coffee.
I want to rest but I sometimes tidy up too.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' introduces a contrasting idea — the speaker wants to rest, yet still tidies up.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the option that connects two ideas that seem to go against each other.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes a relaxed weekend morning?
options: I always leave for work at seven on Saturdays.
I usually sleep in and then go to the market.
I never wake up on weekends.
I work all day and never leave the office.
mcQuestions.explanation: Sleeping in and going to the market is a natural, calm weekend routine.
mcQuestions.hint: Pick the option that sounds like a slow, pleasant morning.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word fits: 'The market sells fresh ___ and fruit.'
options: cars
books
vegetables
computers
mcQuestions.explanation: Markets sell vegetables and fruit — these are food items you find at a market.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about what you find at a food market.

options: I buy oranges because they are cheap.
I buy oranges because cheap.
Because I buy oranges cheap they.
Cheap oranges because I buy.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a subject and a verb: 'because they are cheap'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a full clause with a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option is the correct negative in the present simple?
options: I not work on Sundays.
I don't work on Sundays.
I no work on Sundays.
I am not work on Sundays.
mcQuestions.explanation: Use 'don't' to make a negative with 'I' in the present simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Remember the auxiliary verb for present simple negatives.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'and' to join two actions?
options: I drink coffee and I talk to my friends.
I drink coffee and talking friends.
Drink coffee and I talks friends.
I coffee and friends talk.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'and' joins two complete clauses, each with a subject and a verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Both parts joined by 'and' need their own subject and verb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why is it important to have a relaxing weekend routine, and how does it help you do better during the week?
